source.title: Mooring Line #9 Failure - Titan SPAR Platform MC 941

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 941

event.costs: {"amount": 5000000, "currency": "USD", "description": "Replacement cost estimate including engineering work, vessels, materials"}
actions_taken: Production shut in and POB reduced to essential personnel only
Inspection of remaining 3 mooring lines on Port Column
Attempted to increase tension on ML #9 by hauling in anchor chain
ROV inspection to locate H-link connector and chain components
Recovery of anchor chain, H-link, and adjacent links for failure analysis
Metallurgical failure analysis by Stress Engineering Services Inc.
Production resumed on 7 July 2023 after BSEE approval
event.summary: On 15 June 2023, during a scheduled ABS UWILD inspection at the Titan SPAR platform (MC 941 A), abnormal slack was observed on Mooring Line #9 with a significant length resting on the seabed. The H-link connector was found buried at the seabed. Investigation revealed the 5-inch Grade RQ4 chain link had parted. Metallurgical analysis concluded the failure was most likely due to fatigue initiating at the top of the crown where stresses are high, with corrosion as a contributing factor. Historical data showed ML #9 tension had decreased significantly in March 2022, with low tension alarms noted in September 2022. The remaining 3 mooring lines on the Port Column were inspected and found in good condition, allowing continued manned operations.
recommendations: The BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time
key_phrases: fatigue initiating at the top of the crown

hazards.name: Mooring line under cyclic loading and corrosive environment

threats.name: Fatigue crack initiation at high stress locations (crown)
Corrosion of mooring line chain links
High stress concentration at crown of chain link

controls.name: Scheduled ABS UWILD Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Detection and monitoring of mooring line condition
controls.lod_basis: Periodic inspection program to detect degradation before failure

supporting_text: in accordance with the In-Service Inspection Plan (ISIP), Titan was conducting a scheduled American Bureau of Shipping (ABS) Underwater Inspection in lieu of Drydocking (UWILD)
an abnormal amount of slack was observed on the ML #9

controls.name: Daily Marine Report Tension Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Continuous monitoring of mooring line tension
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring system providing early warning of tension loss

supporting_text: on 11 March 2022, Titan operations found a significant decrease in ML #9 tension recorded on the daily marine report
Six months later on 9 September 2022, low tension and an associated alarm for ML #9 were noted during an internal technical integrity review

controls.name: Mooring Line Tension Alarm System
controls.barrier_role: Automated alarm for low tension conditions
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection and alerting of abnormal conditions

supporting_text: low tension and an associated alarm for ML #9 were noted during an internal technical integrity review

controls.name: Post-Hurricane ROV Inspection
controls.lod_basis: Verification of integrity after known stress event

supporting_text: following Hurricane Ida in August of 2021, an ROV inspection of the mooring lines was completed on 22 December 2021. This report found ML #9 to be in normal condition at that time

controls.name: Maintenance Management Program for Mooring System
controls.barrier_role: Corrective maintenance to address identified degradation
controls.lod_basis: Systematic approach to maintain equipment integrity

supporting_text: Management Systems – Weakness in maintenance management program for mooring system: Reliance on third party vendors to conduct corrective maintenance work orders on the mooring system caused delays due to unavailability

controls.name: Inspection of Remaining Mooring Lines
controls.barrier_role: Verification of remaining station keeping capability
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to assess remaining safety margin

supporting_text: The other 3 MLs on the Port Column were inspected and found to be in good condition. As such, the platform was allowed to continue manned operations
All 3 remaining MLs on the Port Column were found to be in good condition, rendering the platform safe to remain manned

controls.name: Production Shutdown and POB Reduction
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to reduce personnel risk
controls.lod_basis: Immediate action to protect personnel following loss of containment boundary

supporting_text: Equinor shut in production and reduced the number of Personnel on Board (POB) to essential personnel only

controls.name: Material Specification (Grade RQ4/R4 Chain)
controls.barrier_role: Design specification to ensure adequate strength
controls.lod_basis: Material selection to withstand design loads

supporting_text: The link most likely failed due to fatigue initiating at the top of the crown where stresses are high
Tensile properties and chemical composition were acceptable for Grade RQ4/R4 links. Hardness and yield strength were on the high side for Grade R4 and were more typical of Grade R5
